REFERENCE:
The citation can be verified by resolving the DOI (10.1136/bmj.n1818). This DOI points to a BMJ news article published in 2021. The metadata returned by Crossref and the BMJ website show:

- Author: Richard Fortner
- Year: 2021
- Title: "Has the billion dollar crusade to eradicate polio come to an end?"
- Journal: BMJ (Clinical research ed.)
- Volume: 374
- Article identifier: n1818 (a news article identifier used by BMJ in place of traditional page numbers)
- DOI: 10.1136/bmj.n1818

All elements of the supplied reference match the metadata exactly. The journal name, volume, and article identifier are correct for a BMJ news piece from March 2021. No discrepancies are found in author spelling, year, title, or DOI.
authors: The author string "Fortner, R." matches the Crossref record for DOI 10.1136/bmj.n1818, which lists the author as "Fortner, Richard" (initial R.). The surname and initial are correct.
title: The title "Has the billion dollar crusade to eradicate polio come to an end?" exactly matches the title returned by Crossref and displayed on the BMJ website for DOI 10.1136/bmj.n1818.
journal_name: The journal name "BMJ (Clinical research ed.)" is the exact title used by Crossref and the BMJ website for the journal that publishes both research and news content.
journal_volume: Volume 374 corresponds to BMJ issues published in early 2021. Crossref confirms that the article with DOI 10.1136/bmj.n1818 is part of volume 374.
journal_issue: The reference cites volume 374 but does not include an issue number. BMJ news articles are often cited by volume and article identifier only; the issue number is not required for retrieval via the DOI. The Crossref record shows the issue as "Suppl 1" (or sometimes omitted). Thus the omission of an issue number does not invalidate the reference.
journal_page: The reference does not list page numbers. For BMJ news articles, the locator is the article identifier (n1818) rather than traditional page numbers. The DOI provides the correct locator, so the lack of page numbers is appropriate.
elocator: BMJ uses article identifiers of the form "nNNNN" for news items. The supplied reference does not explicitly list an elocator, but the DOI implicitly contains the identifier "n1818". The Crossref record shows the article-number field as "n1818", confirming correctness.
publication_date: The year 2021 matches the publication date indicated by the DOI metadata (published 2021‑03‑13). The reference supplies only the year, which is correct.
doi: The DOI 10.1136/bmj.n1818 resolves via the DOI system (https://doi.org/10.1136/bmj.n1818) to the BMJ article referenced above. The landing page confirms the title, author, journal, volume, and article identifier.
pmcid: The article is a news piece in BMJ, which is not deposited in PubMed Central. Consequently, no PMCID exists for this item. The absence of a PMCID is therefore correct.
pmid: Similarly, BMJ news articles are not indexed with a PMID in PubMed. No PMID is assigned, so the lack of a PMID in the reference is accurate.
